title: ALIGN — 8 Week Full Body Mobility Program

overview.description: Mobility program focusing on seven major joint complexes: ankles, hips, knees, thoracic spine, shoulders, and wrists.
overview.structure: 4 phases of 2 weeks each. Each week has 6 days. 1-2 sets of everything is recommended unless otherwise noted. Sessions take 5-10 minutes.

wrist_routine.description: Bonus routine — complete when needed or before upper body days.

exercises.notes: Make sure to switch which way you are circling half way through.
Keep arms straight.
Switch which way you are circling half way through / with each rep.
Use opposite hand to push to slightly uncomfortable position.
Keep arms straight. Raise palms as high as you can. The goal is to be at least parallel with your knuckles as you extend.
Keep fingers straight, focus on pushing with your palms.
Try to get knees over toes as you sink.
Form as big of circles as you can. Keep hips and shoulders square to the floor.
Hold at top for 2-3 secs, control down.
Hold at top for 2-3 secs, control down.
Place hands behind your back. The goal is to keep your knees as far away from each other as possible as you open.
Try to get knees over your toes with each rep. Don't let db/kb rest on the floor.
Arch spine as much as you can. Inhale as you flex, exhale as you extend.
Aim to get shoulder to the floor. Rotate and reach as much as you can. Sink into your hip as you drop the shoulder.
Arms are extended. The goal is to place opposite foot flat on the floor. As you place your foot, pull extended arm to chest. You want to feel a big stretch there.
Pause at the top of each rep and squeeze as hard as you can. Raise elbows off the floor as high as possible. Slow and controlled.
Notice how palms change positions throughout the movement. Aim to reach as far behind your head/back as you can.
Play around with distance but make sure there is no slack in the band. Squeeze shoulder blades together and pause.
Notice how feet slightly shift positions. Feel free to hold onto a rack for assistance if needed.
Wrap band around kneeling knee's hip. Make sure the extended leg's toes points to the ceiling as you rock back.
Pause for 2-3 secs at the top. Slow and controlled on the way down. Keep hips and shoulders square to floor.
You can hold onto a squat rack / something for assistance. Try to get as low as you can. Point opposite toe to ceiling.
Read description of the YouTube video for in-depth coaching cues.
Wrap band around thumbs and low back. Exhale as you round the back, inhale as you arch the back.
Arms are extended on the floor. Try your best to keep both shoulders on the floor. Make sure there is no space between the floor and your low back.
Bring other shoulder across the chest, drop, and make a full circle. Slow and controlled. Switch which way you are circling.
Arms stay straight. The closer your hands are together, the more difficult the movement is.
Let eyes follow your hand. Try to press entire back onto the wall as you rotate.
Push knees out with elbows. Let eyes follow your hand as you reach.
Try to get knees over your toes with each rep. Make sure to lift the foot off the floor and control with each rep.
Squeeze shoulder blades together, push floor away from you, exhale as you push, and push head through your shoulders as much as you can.
Flow Tip: After you rotate and drop your elbow to the floor, your hand moves right back to where it was behind you.
If alternating is too difficult, do one side/arm at a time! Reach as far back as you can, push hips to the ceiling. Make sure heels are in line with your knees.

days.equipment: wall, plate for deficit, long resistance band

exercises.notes: Back against a wall. Place your feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart, slightly in front of you. Keep your knees straight. Pull toes to nose!
Read description of YouTube video for explanation of this drill.
Up with two feet, down with 1 foot. 3-4 SEC ECCENTRIC TEMPO!
Make sure there's no slack in the band. Flex your quad as much as you can with each rep.

exercises.sets_reps: x10-12 with 20-30 sec hold on last rep
exercises.notes: Keep upright torso. Get knees parallel with hips. Make sure there is no slack between you and the band.

days.equipment: foam roller, bench, mini resistance band

exercises.notes: Sink back and extend arms in front of you.
Make sure knees go over toes. Notice how I shift my back leg and hips as I sink laterally into my hips.
Add mini band around soles of your feet to make more challenging.
90/90 degree positioning. Keep upright torso. Lift off with each rep. Tap the heels.
Keep the core tight with an upright position with your arms extended out in front of you. Keep knees as far away from each other the entire time.

exercises.movement: Elevated Banded Straight Leg Hip Flexion
exercises.notes: Raise hips as high as you can. Squeeze the glutes. Brace your core as you drive your knee to your chest. Pause at the top for 2-3 secs. Keep foot flexed.

days.equipment: foam roller, trx band, mini band

exercises.notes: Form as big of circles as you can. Extend, rotate, crunch.
Notice how I sway side to side to stretch one lat more than the other.
Keep both knees bent and on the floor. Aim to get your entire back on the floor as you rotate.

exercises.movement: SA Childs Pose Lift Off + Y Reach
exercises.notes: Head stays hovering the floor. Notice how I'm extending into a 'Y' position. Lift arms off the floor as much as you can.
Keep constant tension on the band. Small, controlled pulses.

exercises.movement: Assisted Hamstring Stretch + Alt Internal Knee Tap
exercises.notes: Sink back into your hips and extend the spine as you stretch the hamstrings. Control each tap. Try to do without assistance if you can!
Hands follow with you on the floor. Make sure the extended leg's toes point to the ceiling as you switch.
Keep hips and shoulders square to the floor the entire time. Leg is bent at 90 degrees. Form as big of a circle you can and control the leg down onto the bench.

exercises.movement: Bent Knee End Range Hip Internal + External Rotation
exercises.notes: Hold each position at top for 2-3 secs, control down.
Chest stays tall as you sink into your hip as you lunge. Keep non-working leg straight the entire time.
Slow and controlled. Play around with positioning to find the right tension.
Eyes follow your hand. Keep both hands on the wall as you twist.
Keep arms straight as you rotate and reach. Make sure your back knee is off the ground.
Start with one hand on head (palm down), one hand on lower back (palm up). Notice how palms switch the way they are facing.
SMALL CONTROLLED REPS. Thumbs to the ceiling. Try to get arms off the floor as high as you can.

exercises.movement: Couch Stretch + Banded OH Lateral Flexions
exercises.notes: Keep the core and glutes tight. Try your best to get into a straight, upright position where your back heel meets your glute. Tuck the pelvis and slightly sink into your hip to initiate a strong hip stretch. Modify: use lower bench/box.
Hands follow with you on the floor or stability/support.
Keep knees as far away from each other as possible. Keep back flat as you switch and lean forward.
Take the toe touch out if needed and just go into the get up position if needed. It may take a few reps to get used to.
Go down onto your hand first, then progress to your elbow. Keep eyes on weight the entire time. Let elbow or arm glide against your leg.

days.equipment: long resistance band, light weight, plate for deficit

exercises.notes: Squat down as deep as you can. Rock side to side rolling onto the toes, allowing your heels to rock up and down as you sink and let your knees come over the toes. Feel free to place hands on the floor for stability.
Make sure there's no slack in the band. Get knees over toes as much as you can without your heel coming off the floor.
Slow and controlled, pause at the top. Keep foot flexed and pull toes to nose, and then control as the band pulls and points your toes. Make sure there is no slack in band.
2 sec pause at the top, 3 second eccentric tempo. Hold onto something for assistance. Make sure you get a big stretch in the calf. Hold weight if you can.
Feel free to hold a weight / increase height to progress. Slow and controlled. As soon as your heel hits the ground, stand right back up.
Keep chest tall, heels as high off the ground as possible. Get as deep as you can.
Form BIG 'Cs'. Keep core tight, low back glued to the floor. Control, don't just swing.
Make sure the toe points to the ceiling as you drop into the side lunge. Use the floor for assistance. If you need to, drop knee to the floor as you sink forward.
Make sure there is no slack in the band. Let the band pull your knee back as far as your hips will get you. Keep both knees at 90/90. Bring knee all the way back down to the floor.

Make sure your knees are directly under your hips and your hips are directly in line with your torso — forming a vertical line if viewed from the side. Bring leg forward and back in as big of a circle you can. The higher and bigger the circle — the better. Keep knee bent.

Make sure there is no slack in the band, your heel is in line with your knee, squeeze the glutes and raise the hips to the ceiling. Drive knee to chest, pause, and slowly extend the knee back to starting position.

days.equipment: bench, light weight, wall, long resistance band

exercises.notes: Slow reps. Controlled throughout. Imagine you are flexing each vertebrae one at a time.
Notice how I brace my core before I start. Form a fist and let your hand glide against the wall as you rotate the wrist.
Bring elbow to elbow. Try to sink into your hips as you rotate eccentrically.

exercises.movement: Adductor Kneeling Banded Thread the Needle
exercises.notes: Rotate the torso and aim for the hips as you row. You want to feel an extra stretch in your lats as the band assists as you reach through. Try to sit back into the kneeling position to open the hips, too.
Keep pelvis tucked to the wall, elbows bent at 90 degrees, and elbows glued to the wall.

days.equipment: bench, foam roller, mini band, trx

exercises.notes: If a flat bench is too difficult, try using a bench at an incline and do the same move.
Slight pause at the top of the abduction. Control as you come back to starting position.
Slight pause at the top of each extension. Try your best to get your leg as far off the floor as you can. Control on the way down.
Notice how my arms move with each rep. Sink into your hip as deep as you can. CONTROL yourself back down. DO NOT let hips slam to the ground.
You can also mimic this by using a slider instead. Sink into your hip, slight lean forward so nose is over toes. Head stays in line with your body, back stays flat.

days.equipment: foam roller, light weight, bench, long resistance band

exercises.movement: Loaded Extension Over Roller on Bench
exercises.notes: Keep the pelvis on the bench / floor as you extend your upper back over the foam roller and reach overhead with your arms. Aim to get your hands to the floor if you can.
Keep both feet and shoulders on the floor. Play around with positioning of the foam roller on your back.
Sink into your shoulders and move hands behind the head.
Keep knees at 90 degree angle. Aim for your hips and rotate the entire torso as you row. Try to get entire back on the floor as you pull.
Grab a very light weight for these. Squeeze the glutes and raise the arms as high as you can. Extend the arms and squeeze the shoulder blades together eccentrically.
Keep chest tall. Squat as deep as you can. Try NOT to rotate your torso as you internally tap the knee.
Use the floor for assistance if needed.

Start in 90/90 position. Lift off with each rep. Keep leg off the ground until you control the heel to the floor. Notice how my arms flow with the movement. Sink into the hips, then CONTROL back to starting position.
Keep knees as far apart as possible. Keep upright torso. Squeeze the glutes as you reach behind you.
Go down onto your hand, then progress to your elbow if needed. Keep eyes on the weight the entire time. Let elbow or arm glide against your leg. Sink into your hips on the way down.

days.equipment: mini band, elevation, mini + long band

exercises.notes: Pause at the top, slowly control on the way down.
Do not let hips raise as you raise. Focus on slow and controlled reps. Pause at the top. Stay as low as you can throughout.

exercises.movement: Assisted Tip Toe Deep Squat Knee Drops
exercises.notes: Stay on the toes. CONTROL your knees to the floor by keeping chest to the ceiling and core tight. Stay as low as you can.

exercises.sets_reps: x3-5 each side (all three taps = 1 rep)
exercises.notes: Control each rep on the way down, tap the heel, and stand right back up. Balance and reach out as far as you can with each tap. Front, lateral, reverse.

exercises.sets_reps: x3-5 each side (all three taps = 1 rep)
exercises.notes: Make sure there is no slack between you and the band. 3-4 second eccentric tempo. DO NOT let the heel touch the ground. Tap the toe and stand straight back up.
Push knee out laterally at the bottom for even more of a stretch.
Use a wall to lean against. Keep upright torso, make as big of circles as you can. Make sure to switch which way you are circling.

exercises.movement: Quadruped End Range Lift Off + Knee Extension
exercises.notes: Keep shoulders and hips square to the floor throughout. Bend the knee on the bench, lift off as high as you can, and extend the leg. Control back down to bench.
Bend the knee, drive up, bring knee out to the side as high as you can, and control slowly down to the floor. Lift off and repeat in the opposite direction — forming as big of a circle you can.

exercises.movement: Elevated Pause Standing KB Hip Flexion
exercises.notes: 2-3 sec pause at the top. Drive knee as high as you can. You must keep your foot flexed to hold the weight. No hunching or rounding of the back as you drive.

days.equipment: long band, bench, light weight, trx

exercises.notes: Make sure to tuck the chin and round the back, then try your best to flatten out as you extend.
Rotate and reach just until it's slightly uncomfortable. Aim to get further with each rep. Do not rush it.
Let hand glide against the floor as you form a rainbow. Try your best to keep the bent knee on the floor.
Keep arms straight the entire time. Raise into a 'Y' position, pause, and slowly control down.
Keep hips tucked under your torso — glutes engaged, core tight. Keep arms straight the entire time, sink into your shoulders until you are at least parallel with your head.

days.equipment: slider, bench, mini band, light weight

exercises.notes: Feel free to do this with an incline bench instead of a flat bench. Keep chest upright, back flat. Tap the knee on the floor.

Reach as far as you can, aiming to form a straight line with your head, arms, and hips. Sink into your hip and keep a slight bend in the knees. Pause at the bottom and really focus on extending as much as you can.
Sink as far as you can laterally as you keep the leg straight. Keep upright torso. Can use a towel and hard wood floor if you do not have a slider.
Pause at the top of each rep, slow and controlled on the way back eccentrically.
Hold weight to your chest. Slow and controlled. Stay as low as you can, remember to point toes to the ceiling as you switch.
Think about rounding the spine, tucking the chin, go down, twist, rotate, twist, etc.

exercises.movement: Hand on Back Quadruped Elbow Spine Rotation
exercises.notes: Opening up as much as you can. Let eyes follow as you rotate. Reach elbow to the ceiling.
Start with thumbs facing the ceiling, then rotate the wrist as you circulate. Keep working arm STRAIGHT and hips on the floor.
Focus on keeping your elbows on the wall to get the most out of this movement. Twisting all the way forward and all the way back.
If standing is too difficult at first, lay on the ground with your knees bent and feet flat. Maintain an upright torso and keep elbows glued to the wall as you extend overhead.
Using some hip mobility and power here. Aim to get your foot beside the hand. Open up and rotate towards the leg that's on the ground.
Using your hands to assist you into the tripod position. Raise hips as high as you can as you reach behind you as far as you can.
Use floor for assistance. Stay slow and controlled.
Start with one leg extended in front of you and the other tucked under the opposite leg. Use your hips to get up and sink into your hip / ankle. Notice how I position my arms throughout the movement.
Try to find a flow. As you press, your hips are extending. As you descend, your hips are descending with you at the same time. Eyes stay on the weight the entire time.

Anchor the mini band to your feet flat on the ground and then bend one leg and place on top of opposite leg. SLOW AND CONTROLLED. Make as big of circles as you can. Make sure to switch which way you're circling.

exercises.sets_reps: x3-5 each side (all three taps = 1 rep)
exercises.notes: Reach as far as you can with each angle. Tap the heel.
Make sure to get a big enough stretch in the calf with the deficit. Let back knee hover the ground. Do not let hips raise as you flex the calf.
Read description of YouTube video for in-depth coaching cues.

Stay as low as you can. Maintain an upright torso with a tall chest. Make sure your feet travel in a vertical line — try not to let feet go out laterally as you step. Make sure your knees and toes stay pointed forward.
